toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run the tests with `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. 

For .NET, crash dumps can be collected using environment variables that configure the .NET runtime's crash dump generation. The key environment variables are:

- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables mini dump collection on crash
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — Full dump (1=Mini, 2=Heap, 3=Triage, 4=Full)
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=<path>` — path/pattern for the dump file


Now I'll set up these environment variables and run the test command to capture any crash dumps that occur.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-fd9d24bd8f4b479abaceece39e77365e && \\\n  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=\"/tmp/sv-fd9d24bd8f4b479abaceece39e77365e/crashdump_%p.dmp\" \\\n  dotnet test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's the command to run with crash dump collection:

```bash
DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="/tmp/sv-fd9d24bd8f4b479abaceece39e77365e/crashdump_%p.dmp" \
dotnet test
```

**What the environment variables do:**

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` | Enables automatic dump on unhandled crash |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` | Captures a **full** dump (use `1` for a smaller mini dump) |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` | Output path (`%p` = process ID) |

No crashes occurred in this run, so no dump was generated. If a test ever crashes the host process, a `.dmp` file will be written to this directory.
